title: Remove the pneumatic valve cover and disconnect the valve electrical connector
summary: Initial pneumatic valve removal actions for the OptiSweep tipper: gather the specified replacement part, PPE, and tools; apply lockout/tagout to the tipper; remove the station side panel if needed for access; remove the valve cover fasteners; and disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the valve.
when_to_use: Use when performing the source-documented opening steps of pneumatic valve replacement/removal on the OptiSweep tipper and access is needed to the pneumatic valve cover and valve electrical connector.
not_for: Do not use as a complete pneumatic valve replacement procedure; the supplied source packet only supports the opening removal steps.
Do not use if lockout/tagout of the tipper cannot be completed as referenced by the source.
Do not use if guarded-area or service access cannot be safely obtained.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, access panel removal if needed, valve cover fastener removal, and M8 electrical connector disconnection.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before beginning removal work.
Stop if LOTO cannot be completed as referenced by the source.
Stop if guarded-area or service access cannot be safely obtained.

access_required: Service access to the station side panel and valve area
system_preconditions: Tipper can be locked out/tagged out before removal begins

visual_references.description: Figure 7-9 Pneumatic Valve; use for pneumatic valve location, valve cover fasteners, and M8 connector identification.

steps.title: Gather replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required materials and tools are available before beginning the valve removal task.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement pneumatic valve, safety glasses, gloves, 2.5-mm hex wrench, 3-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite.
steps.expected_result: All listed PPE, tools, and the replacement pneumatic valve are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: Required PPE, tools, and replacement part are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required items are missing or unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the required replacement part, PPE, and tools: pneumatic valve, safety glasses, gloves, 2.5-mm hex wrench, 3-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall pneumatic valve assembly to orient the service task before beginning removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required PPE, tools, or replacement pneumatic valve are not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the required replacement part, PPE, and tools: pneumatic valve, safety glasses, gloves, 2.5-mm hex wrench, 3-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite.

Nearby procedure text lists required tools and PPE, including safety glasses, gloves, 2.5-mm, 3-mm, 5-mm, and 6-mm hex wrenches, a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite, with an estimated time of 45 minutes.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the tipper in a safe service state before opening access panels or disconnecting components.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper as referenced by Operator Station Lockout/Tagout on page 2 before continuing.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out/tagged out and ready for safe service access.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been completed in accordance with the referenced source procedure.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO cannot be completed or the tipper is not in a safe locked-out state.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: LOTO cannot be completed as referenced by the source.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.
Stop if LOTO cannot be completed as referenced by the source.
The removal steps around this figure instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if access is needed
steps.purpose: Open access to the valve area when the side panel blocks service access.
steps.instruction: If necessary for access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed and the valve area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: Side panel is removed cleanly and service access to the valve area is obtained.
steps.failure_condition: Guarded-area or service access cannot be safely obtained, or the side panel cannot be removed as described.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove station side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Valve area orientation after side panel access is opened.

stop_or_escalate_if: Guarded-area or service access cannot be safely obtained.
The side panel cannot be removed as described.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
Stop if guarded-area or service access cannot be safely obtained.
The removal steps around this figure instruct the technician to remove the station side panel if needed.

steps.title: Remove the valve cover screws
steps.purpose: Open the valve cover so the valve and connector can be accessed.
steps.instruction: Remove the four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover in place.
steps.expected_result: The four valve cover screws are removed and the valve cover is free to be taken off.
steps.healthy_condition: All four M4 socket-head screws are removed and the valve cover is no longer secured.
steps.failure_condition: One or more valve cover screws cannot be removed or the cover remains secured.

actions.label: Remove valve cover screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Valve cover and the four M4 socket-head screws on the pneumatic valve assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: The valve cover fasteners cannot be identified or removed as described.

source_evidence.text: Remove the four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover in place.
The removal steps around this figure instruct the technician to remove four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-9 appears on page 144 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_9_1_pneumatic_valve_removal.

steps.title: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector
steps.purpose: Separate the valve electrical connection as part of the documented opening removal sequence.
steps.instruction: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.
steps.expected_result: The M8 electrical connector is disconnected from the valve.
steps.healthy_condition: The connector is fully disconnected from the valve edge connection point.
steps.failure_condition: The M8 electrical connector cannot be identified or disconnected.

actions.label: Disconnect valve connector
actions.exact_action: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: M8 electrical connector location at the edge of the valve.

stop_or_escalate_if: The M8 electrical connector cannot be identified or disconnected as described.

source_evidence.text: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.
The removal steps around this figure instruct the technician to disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.

success_criteria: The tipper is locked out/tagged out.
The station side panel is removed if needed for access.
The valve cover fasteners are removed.
The M8 electrical connector is disconnected from the valve.
healthy_conditions: Required PPE, tools, and replacement pneumatic valve are available before work begins.
LOTO is completed before service access is opened.
The valve area is accessible after side panel removal if needed.
The valve cover is unsecured after removal of the four M4 socket-head screws.
The M8 electrical connector is disconnected from the valve.
failure_conditions: LOTO cannot be completed as referenced by the source.
Guarded-area or service access cannot be safely obtained.
The valve cover fasteners cannot be removed as described.
The M8 electrical connector cannot be disconnected.
The source packet does not include the full replacement or reinstallation sequence.
escalation_guidance: Stop the procedure if LOTO cannot be completed as referenced by the source.
Stop the procedure if guarded-area or service access cannot be safely obtained.
Escalate for SME/manual review if additional pneumatic valve replacement or reinstallation steps are required, because the supplied source content is partial.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pneumatic valve location, valve cover fasteners, and M8 electrical connector at the valve edge.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-9 appears on page 144 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_9_1_pneumatic_valve_removal.

missing_details: The supplied source packet does not include the full pneumatic valve replacement or reinstallation sequence.
The source sections in this packet contain no OCR text, so step wording is grounded primarily in the candidate and artifact retrieval summaries.
The packet does not explicitly identify which exact hex wrench size is used for each fastener in these opening steps.
The packet does not explicitly state whether production stop is required beyond LOTO.
The packet does not provide a detailed post-disconnection verification step.
